Title: Pathogen promotes insect ovarian development by inducing a dual role of host ATPSyn-β

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Vitellogenin family protein with LPD_N, DUF1943, and VWD domains
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: On and in oocytes; associated with oocyte membrane uptake
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in CLas+ ovaries; strongly reduced after ATPSyn-β knockdown
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in CLas+ ovaries than CLas- ovaries
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidatus Liberibacter asiaticus infection; ATPSyn-β RNAi
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ovarian nutrient accumulation, contingent on ATPSyn-β-mediated uptake
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Vg was identified as a reproduction-related DEG in CLas+ ovaries. 2. Vg protein interacted with ATPSyn-β, specifically through its VWD domain. 3. Vg co-localized with ATPSyn-β on and in oocytes. 4. ATPSyn-β knockdown reduced Vg transcript and protein abundance in ovaries, consistent with impaired nutrient delivery to oocytes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; RT-qPCR; co-immunoprecipitation LC-MS/MS; yeast two-hybrid; GST pull-down; immunofluorescence; Western blot after ATPSyn-β RNAi
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential indirect target in vector control through disruption of ovarian nutrient uptake pathways
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study treats Vg mainly as the transported nutrient factor rather than the primary causal core gene; however it is experimentally central to the nutrient-supply mechanism.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increased after silencing of ATPSyn-α or ATPSyn-β
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in fat body after RNAi treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: RNAi silencing of ATPSyn-α and/or ATPSyn-β
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker of altered lipid mobilization pathway associated with reduced triglyceride content after ATP synthase knockdown
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AKH expression increased after ATPSyn-α or ATPSyn-β knockdown. 2. This increase accompanied reduced glycogen and triglyceride content in the fat body. 3. AKH was used as a key lipid-metabolism readout rather than a primary manipulated gene.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR after RNAi treatment; glycogen/triglyceride assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a common metabolism-related gene measured in the study, but not a primary experimental target.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides cellular energy for ovarian development, mating, and oviposition
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mitochondrial ATP synthesis / oxidative phosphorylation; also moonlighting membrane-associated nutrient transport module
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product; also required for nutrient uptake linked to ovarian development
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ADP; inorganic phosphate; proton motive force
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalytic beta subunit of F1Fo-ATP synthase that synthesizes ATP from ADP and inorganic phosphate using the proton gradient
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mitochondria; oocyte cell membrane; microsomal fraction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in ovaries of CLas-infected females, especially at 4 and 6 days post-emergence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in CLas+ ovaries than CLas- ovaries
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidatus Liberibacter asiaticus infection; RNAi feeding with dsATPSyn-β
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ATP accumulation in ovary, increases glycogen and triglyceride availability, enhances vitellogenin entry into oocytes, and thereby promotes ovarian development, mating, and oviposition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CLas infection upregulated ATPSyn-β expression in D. citri ovaries. 2. RNAi silencing of ATPSyn-β reduced ovarian ATP content and decreased glycogen and triglyceride content in the fat body. 3. ATPSyn-β knockdown impaired ovarian development, reduced mating rate, and lowered egg production. 4. ATPSyn-β localized to the oocyte membrane and interacted with the VWD domain of Vg, supporting Vg uptake into oocytes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative ovary transcriptomics; RT-qPCR; RNAi knockdown by feeding; Western blot; ATP assay; glycogen and triglyceride assays; immunofluorescence localization; co-immunoprecipitation LC-MS/MS; yeast two-hybrid; GST pull-down; mating and oviposition phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Vg; ATPSyn-α; myosin heavy chain; actin; apolipophorin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for ovarian energy supply and nutrient uptake; not explicitly established as rate-limiting for oxidative phosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher reproduction associated with decreased female longevity; high dsATPSyn-β concentrations can kill D. citri
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for pest control strategies to reduce D. citri reproduction and thereby limit citrus Huanglongbing spread; RNAi-based suppression is particularly promising
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ATPSyn-β shows dual functionality: canonical ATP synthesis role and noncanonical membrane-associated transporter-like role facilitating Vg uptake into oocytes. The paper emphasizes ATPSyn-β over ATPSyn-α due to stronger inhibitory phenotype after silencing.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides cellular energy for ovarian development and reproduction
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for ATP synthesis complex that directly forms target product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ADP; inorganic phosphate; proton motive force
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Noncatalytic/core F1 ATP synthase alpha subunit required for ATP synthase complex function in ATP production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in CLas+ ovaries compared with CLas- ovaries
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in CLas+ ovaries than CLas- ovaries
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidatus Liberibacter asiaticus infection; RNAi feeding with dsATPSyn-α
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ATP production and is required for normal ovarian development, mating behavior, and oviposition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATPSyn-α was among oxidative phosphorylation genes upregulated in CLas+ ovaries. 2. RNAi silencing of ATPSyn-α reduced ATP content in ovaries. 3. ATPSyn-α knockdown decreased glycogen and triglyceride content, impaired ovarian development, and reduced mating rate and egg production. 4. ATPSyn-α did not show significant interaction with Vg according to the discussion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative ovary transcriptomics; RT-qPCR; RNAi knockdown by feeding; Western blot; ATP assay; glycogen and triglyceride assays; mating and oviposition phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key ATP synthase complex component; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces reproduction; simultaneous silencing with ATPSyn-β abolished egg production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential RNAi pest-control target, though the paper prioritizes ATPSyn-β because of stronger phenotype and dual role
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although classified here as pathway gene due to oxidative phosphorylation assignment, the alpha subunit is primarily structural/regulatory within the ATP synthase complex rather than the direct catalytic subunit.
